title: Fair Representation Amendment
plain_summary: This bill tells fifteen different House committees to look at all the laws they work with and find places where the Executive Branch has too much power to make decisions on their own. Each committee has six months to suggest changes that would give Congress more control over how laws are used. After that, the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform must write a new bill called the Article One Restoration Act that includes all of these suggestions. The goal is to make sure Congress has more say in how laws work instead of letting the Executive Branch decide everything.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Rules.
subjects: House Committee on Education and Workforce
House Committee on Energy and Commerce
House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform
House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology
House Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure
House Committee on Ways and Means
House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This resolution can be called the Fair Representation Amendment.

sections.title: Section 2 Authorizing Committee Actions
sections.text: Within six months after this resolution is approved, the committees listed below must do two things. First, they must review all the laws that fall under their area of responsibility. Second, they must send recommendations to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform about how to change those laws. The goal is to reduce cases where the Executive Branch (the President and federal agencies) has too much freedom to decide how to enforce or apply those laws. The committees that must do this work are the Committee on Agriculture, Committee on Armed Services, Committee on the Budget, Committee on Energy and Commerce, Committee on Education and the Workforce, Committee on Financial Services, Committee on Foreign Affairs, Committee on the Judiciary, Committee on Natural Resources, Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, Committee on Science Space and Technology, Committee on Small Business, Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure, Committee on Veterans Affairs, Committee on Ways and Means, and the Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence.

sections.title: Section 3 Legislative Procedure
sections.text: After the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform receives all the recommendations from the other committees, they must quickly create a bill that includes all those recommendations. They cannot make any major changes to what the other committees suggested. This new bill must be called the Article One Restoration Act.
